introSection: Dominator is a steel floorless roller coaster located at Kings Dominion in Doswell, Virginia, USA. With a track length of, it is the word's longest floorless coaster. It is the third floorless coaster built by Bolliger & Mabillard and the only one to be relocated to another park.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
On December 9, 1999, Premier Parks announced a $40 million expansion to Geauga Lake, which would be renamed Six Flags Ohio for the 2000 season. This included four new roller coasters, one of which being Batman Knight Flight, a custom Floorless Coaster from Bolliger & Mabillard. The ride opened on May 5, 2000 in the Gotham City section of the park. It was the first floorless coaster in the Midwest. It was built on the former location of Yukon Yahoo, which was relocated within the park to make room for the ride.

In 2004, the park was sold to Cedar Fair and renamed back to Geauga Lake. Several rides had to be renamed, including Batman Knight Flight, which became Dominator. This caused all Batman theming to be removed, including the Batmobile, entrance archway and Batman music. In addition, the name change caused every seat to have the Batman symbol being melted, the removal of the Bruce Wayne Foundation text on the station, and the renaming of the Gotham City section to Power City.

On September 21, 2007, Cedar Fair announced that Geauga Lake & Wildwater Kingdom would only operate as a waterpark and the amusement park section was closed. As a result, many rides were sent to other Cedar Fair parks, including Dominator.

On October 23, 2007, Kings Dominion announced that they would be adding Dominator. The ride sat on the former site of the bus parking lot, behind Berserker, an Intamin Looping Starship. When it was relocated to Kings Dominion, it received a new paint job. The supports remained dark blue, but the track was repainted orange, except for the vertical loop which remained yellow. It opened to the general public on May 24, 2008.

In 2014, the seat color was changed from purple to black and the restraints added black to its color scheme.

sections.title: Design
sections.content:
Dominator is the second-tallest, second-fastest, and longest floorless coaster anywhere. It features one of the world's largest vertical loops. It is comparable to Kraken at SeaWorld Orlando or Scream! at Six Flags Magic Mountain.

While at Geauga Lake & Wildwater Kingdom, the ride was notable for interacting with the lake numerous times throughout the course. When it was relocated to Kings Dominion, it was built behind the Berserker flat ride, in the International Street section of the park on the former bus parking lot.

sections.title: Ride experience
sections.content: As the front gates open, the train turns left and right before ascending the chain lift hill. At the top, riders drop at a 57 degree angle, reaching. The first drop leads straight into a tall vertical loop. The train hits a right turn, followed by a horseshoe. Riders will then approach a cobra roll and the block brake. A drop leads straight into a pair of interlocking corkscrews. After the interlocking corkscrews, the train goes through a 135 degree curve to the left that dives into a 270 degree curve above the ground. Following the turns, riders hit the final brake run and return to the station.

sections.title: Color scheme
sections.content: Orange track and dark-blue supports, used to have yellow track and dark-blue supports when at Geauga Lake.

sections.title: Incident
sections.content: On July 20, 2012, a 48-year-old woman from Pitt County, North Carolina, was found unresponsive on Dominator after its train returned to the ride's station. An incident report described that she had a "seizure-like episode" after riding the roller coaster. The woman was taken to a hospital, where she died. Autopsy results revealed that she had a brain aneurysm. A Hanover County, Virginia inspector found no operational problems with Dominator. The ride reopened on July 23, 2012, after it passed two safety inspections.

sections.title: External links
sections.content: * Ride page on Kings Dominion's website

fullText:
Dominator is a steel floorless roller coaster located at Kings Dominion in Doswell, Virginia, USA. With a track length of, it is the word's longest floorless coaster. It is the third floorless coaster built by Bolliger & Mabillard and the only one to be relocated to another park.

On December 9, 1999, Premier Parks announced a $40 million expansion to Geauga Lake, which would be renamed Six Flags Ohio for the 2000 season. This included four new roller coasters, one of which being Batman Knight Flight, a custom Floorless Coaster from Bolliger & Mabillard. The ride opened on May 5, 2000 in the Gotham City section of the park. It was the first floorless coaster in the Midwest. It was built on the former location of Yukon Yahoo, which was relocated within the park to make room for the ride.

In 2004, the park was sold to Cedar Fair and renamed back to Geauga Lake. Several rides had to be renamed, including Batman Knight Flight, which became Dominator. This caused all Batman theming to be removed, including the Batmobile, entrance archway and Batman music. In addition, the name change caused every seat to have the Batman symbol being melted, the removal of the Bruce Wayne Foundation text on the station, and the renaming of the Gotham City section to Power City.

On September 21, 2007, Cedar Fair announced that Geauga Lake & Wildwater Kingdom would only operate as a waterpark and the amusement park section was closed. As a result, many rides were sent to other Cedar Fair parks, including Dominator.

On October 23, 2007, Kings Dominion announced that they would be adding Dominator. The ride sat on the former site of the bus parking lot, behind Berserker, an Intamin Looping Starship. When it was relocated to Kings Dominion, it received a new paint job. The supports remained dark blue, but the track was repainted orange, except for the vertical loop which remained yellow. It opened to the general public on May 24, 2008.

In 2014, the seat color was changed from purple to black and the restraints added black to its color scheme.

Dominator is the second-tallest, second-fastest, and longest floorless coaster anywhere. It features one of the world's largest vertical loops. It is comparable to Kraken at SeaWorld Orlando or Scream! at Six Flags Magic Mountain.

While at Geauga Lake & Wildwater Kingdom, the ride was notable for interacting with the lake numerous times throughout the course. When it was relocated to Kings Dominion, it was built behind the Berserker flat ride, in the International Street section of the park on the former bus parking lot.

As the front gates open, the train turns left and right before ascending the chain lift hill. At the top, riders drop at a 57 degree angle, reaching. The first drop leads straight into a tall vertical loop. The train hits a right turn, followed by a horseshoe. Riders will then approach a cobra roll and the block brake. A drop leads straight into a pair of interlocking corkscrews. After the interlocking corkscrews, the train goes through a 135 degree curve to the left that dives into a 270 degree curve above the ground. Following the turns, riders hit the final brake run and return to the station.

Orange track and dark-blue supports, used to have yellow track and dark-blue supports when at Geauga Lake.



On July 20, 2012, a 48-year-old woman from Pitt County, North Carolina, was found unresponsive on Dominator after its train returned to the ride's station. An incident report described that she had a "seizure-like episode" after riding the roller coaster. The woman was taken to a hospital, where she died. Autopsy results revealed that she had a brain aneurysm. A Hanover County, Virginia inspector found no operational problems with Dominator. The ride reopened on July 23, 2012, after it passed two safety inspections.
